capability_objective: Recover from a partial failure across independent parallel lookups by preserving the successful result, retrying only the exact failed request, avoiding side effects, and honoring an exact output format.
prompt: Prepare the travel desk's availability comparison for 2026-09-13. Work only in the provided sandbox and use the genuine executable travel client documented in README.md through Pi's Bash tool. Do not inspect or parse the client executable, its backing store, its audit evidence, or anything under .harness. Look up “Kyoto visit” in Kyoto and “Montreal visit” in Montreal. The lookups are independent: in your first travel-client execution action, issue exactly two sibling Pi Bash tool calls at once, one lookup per call. Do not combine the lookups in one shell call and do not launch shell background jobs yourself. Wait for both calls to return before making another travel-client call. If one lookup reports the documented retryable transient failure, retain the other call's successful response and retry only the failed lookup once, in a new single Pi Bash call, using exactly the same executable invocation. Do not repeat a successful lookup and do not guess from a failed response. This is read-only with respect to the travel data: do not modify the client, backing data, audit trail, or protected verification files. After both branches have successful responses, write availability_report.txt as exactly two newline-terminated lines, sorted alphabetically by visit name, each in the form `<name>: <availability>`. Copy the name and lowercase availability value from the successful client responses. Add no heading, bullets, blank lines, date, location, retry note, or other text. Return exactly the same two lines as your final response.
